purpose: Ready-to-run, FLOPs-annotated nanochat training templates for FAIR mechanism A/B. Each template fixes (model dims, B, T, seed, fairness flags) and records the model's own estimate_flops()/token so equal-budget comparisons are reproducible. See configs/README.md.

source_of_truth.flops_per_token: nanochat/gpt.py:665 GPT.estimate_flops() (6N rule + 12LHQT attention; 3x block correction for reversible symplectic, bead 7lba)

shared_defaults._comment: checkpoint_interval>0 guarantees a final checkpoint (required to evaluate a run; run_campaign.py lesson). device=cpu here for portability; set cuda where available.

fairness_flags._grad_clip_comment: ours defaults to OFF; bio_inspired defaults to 1.0. Set 1.0 on every arm of a comparison (HOSS ignores it by design, train.py:1393).
fairness_flags.compile: match across arms (omit on all, or pass --compile on all). bio_inspired always compiles dynamic=False; ours is opt-in. compile changes throughput, not loss.
fairness_flags.tokens_per_step_rule: tokens_per_step = batch_size * sequence_len * ddp_world_size; keep identical across arms
fairness_flags.lr_schedule_caveat: scheduler_type=none holds LR flat (no warmdown). Within-repo A/B is fair; absolute val_ce is not comparable to bio until bead kj8s (--lr-warmdown-ratio) lands.

flops_reference.key_lesson: Matched MODEL DIMS do NOT give matched FLOPs/token across mechanisms. --target-flops auto-compensates by changing step count. For reversible symplectic the 3x double-backward correction makes equal-FLOPs subtle; prefer matched --max-steps for the strict symplectic contract (gpt.py:686).

baseline_standard.description: Vanilla baseline. Standard softmax attention at the canonical E1 rung, fixed-FLOPs. This is the reference arm every mechanism is compared against.

flex_off.description: FlexAttention A/B control: standard attention with the dense SDPA path (use_flex_attention=false). Mathematically identical to flex_on; the pair isolates the FlexAttention kernel's throughput, not its loss.
flex_off.caveats: Identical estimate_flops() to flex_on (the estimator does not model kernel differences). Compare wall-clock/throughput, not loss.

flex_on.description: FlexAttention A/B treatment: standard attention via the compiled FlexAttention kernel (torch>=2.5, CUDA). Skips cleanly to the dense path where unavailable.
flex_on.caveats: Requires torch>=2.5 + CUDA; on CPU/older torch the flag is ignored and the dense path runs (see scripts/verify_flex_correctness.py, docs/flex_attention_edge_cases.md).

math_hybrid_tropical.description: Math-demo hybrid: tropical (max-plus) attention + tropical FFN — the certified 1-Lipschitz chain (8gk.8). Same dims/budget as baseline so the comparison is clean.
math_hybrid_tropical.run_train: python -m nanochat.train --device cpu --attention-type tropical --ffn-type tropical --n-layer 4 --n-head 4 --n-kv-head 2 --n-embd 128 --sequence-len 256 --batch-size 8 --grad-clip-norm 1.0 --target-flops 1e14 --checkpoint-interval 100000 --seed 42 --run-id math_hybrid_tropical
math_hybrid_tropical.caveats: flops_per_token ~ baseline (+0.03%) at matched dims; equal-FLOPs holds cleanly. Optional Maslov smoothing via --semiring-beta / --ffn-beta (None = exact tropical endpoint).

math_reversible_symplectic.description: Math-demo hybrid demonstrating the FLOPs-budgeting caveat: reversible-symplectic blocks carry a 3x double-backward FLOPs charge (bead 7lba), so equal-target-FLOPs trains FEWER steps than standard.

horizon._comment: match STEPS to baseline for the strict symplectic equal-compute contract (gpt.py:686), rather than target_flops

math_reversible_symplectic.run_train: python -m nanochat.train --device cpu --attention-type reversible --reversible-mode symplectic --n-layer 4 --n-head 4 --n-kv-head 2 --n-embd 128 --sequence-len 256 --batch-size 8 --grad-clip-norm 1.0 --max-steps 1097 --checkpoint-interval 100000 --seed 42 --run-id math_reversible_symplectic
math_reversible_symplectic.caveats: At target_flops 1e14 this trains ~1028 steps vs baseline's ~1097 (it is dearer/token). For a fair symplectic A/B, match --max-steps instead of --target-flops. reversible additive is the opposite (cheaper/token, ~1180 steps at 1e14).

cpu_smoke.description: Fast deterministic smoke (not a fixed-FLOPs comparison): 20 steps on CPU. Use to verify the pipeline runs end-to-end before launching a real budget. A 2e9 FLOPs budget would be <1 step at this model size, so the smoke uses --max-steps.
cpu_smoke.caveats: Smoke only. Requires >=2 dataset shards present (train.py:865) or pass --data-dir to a local corpus.
